text:
## Team Context

**Your assigned name**: spec-developer-editor-extensions
**Team Lead**: team-lead (use SendMessage to reach it)

**Communication Protocol** (you have SendMessage in your tools):
- `QUESTION: ...` — before starting, if requirements have genuine ambiguity
- `BLOCKER: ...` — immediately if you cannot proceed
- `DONE: {summary}` — when your deliverables are complete
- `SUGGESTION: ...` — proactively flag tech issues, arch concerns, refactoring ideas

**Context Strategy**: repomix
All relevant context is pre-loaded. Use Read/Glob/Grep for additional files.

**Escalation rule**: Do NOT work silently on ambiguity. Ask first.
**Team culture**: Your domain expertise is valued. Speak up.

## Tasks (5 tasks, do them all sequentially)

### Task 1: notesaner-01n — KaTeX Math Block Extension
LaTeX math rendering in editor using KaTeX.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Inline math: $formula$ renders inline
- Block math: $$formula$$ renders as display block
- KaTeX rendering in read mode
- Edit raw LaTeX in source mode
- Error display for invalid LaTeX
- Slash command: /math to insert math block
- Keyboard shortcut: Ctrl+M for inline math
- Common math symbols autocomplete

### Task 2: notesaner-6n0 — Block Drag Handle for Reordering
Six-dots drag handle on block hover for repositioning blocks.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Handle appears on hover of any block-level element
- Click-drag to reorder blocks within note
- Drop indicator line shows target position
- Works for: paragraphs, headings, lists, code blocks, tables, images
- Cross-pane drag (drag block to another open note) -- optional
- Touch support (long press on mobile) -- optional

### Task 3: notesaner-k73m — Editor Source Mode (Raw Markdown)
Raw Markdown source editing mode alongside WYSIWYG.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Toggle between WYSIWYG (default), Live Preview, and Source mode
- Source mode: plain text textarea/CodeMirror editor with Markdown syntax highlighting
- Sync between modes: switching modes updates content
- Live Preview mode: show rendered preview alongside source
- Source mode preserves exact Markdown including custom syntax
- Keyboard shortcut: Cmd+E cycles modes

### Task 4: notesaner-1vna — Note Reading Mode (Clean View)
Clean, distraction-free reading view for notes.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- Toggle between edit and read mode (Cmd+E)
- Read mode: rendered HTML, no editor chrome
- Adjustable: font size, line height, content width, font family
- Typography optimized for reading (larger size, more line-height)
- No cursor, no editor handles
- Still shows: title, breadcrumb, word count
- Comments visible in read mode
- Back to edit mode with click or keyboard

### Task 5: notesaner-m38 — Note Embed/Transclusion Extension (![[Note]])
TipTap extension for embedding/transcluding other notes inline.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- ![[Note Title]] renders inline preview of target note
- ![[image.png]] renders image
- Live content: transcluded note shows current content
- Nested embeds prevented (max depth: 1 level)
- Click on embed navigates to source note
- Circular embed detection and prevention
- Lazy loading of embed content
- Fallback for missing notes (placeholder)

## Project Info
- TipTap (ProseMirror), React 19
- Project root: /Users/zeizel/projects/notesaner
- Editor core: libs/editor-core/src/
- Extensions: libs/editor-core/src/extensions/
- Components: libs/editor-core/src/components/
- NoteEditor: libs/editor-core/src/components/NoteEditor.tsx
- Main editor component: apps/web/src/features/editor/
- Extensions index: libs/editor-core/src/extensions/index.ts
- Tests: libs/editor-core/src/__tests__/

## Key Architecture Decisions
- TipTap StarterKit as base
- Extensions registered via getBaseExtensions() in extensions.ts
- NoteEditor.tsx is the React wrapper component
- Slash commands already implemented (slash-command.ts)
- Wiki links already implemented (wiki-link.ts)
- Image embed already implemented (image-embed.ts) -- follow same patterns
- Use ProseMirror plugins for low-level behavior
- Source/Reading mode components go in apps/web/src/features/editor/

Install KaTeX package if needed: `pnpm add katex @types/katex` in appropriate package.
Write unit tests for extensions.
summary: KaTeX math + block drag + source mode + reading mode + embed/transclusion
